qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via unspecified IP address (0.0.0.0) bypass in audio host validator
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies a missing IsUnspecified() check in server/audio/host_validator.go:57 as the root cause, which is an SSRF via IP validation bypass. The advisory describes missing authentication on REST and WebSocket routes (CWE-306). These are different root causes: the advisory fix adds LINKDAVE_PASSWORD authentication, while the SSRF fix would add IsUnspecified() to the host validator. The SSRF finding leverages the unauthenticated WebSocket as part of its attack path, but the vulnerable code and security boundary it identifies are distinct from the advisory-described missing authentication.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: missing authentication on REST and WebSocket routes in linkdave (CWE-306). The ssrf-via-zero-ip-bypass finding identifies a different vulnerability (missing IsUnspecified() check in host_validator.go) in the same repository.
evidence: Advisory describes missing authentication (CWE-306) on REST/WebSocket routes, fixed by adding LINKDAVE_PASSWORD authentication in commit 0f9a00d9d549b16278db81fce6dfec350c2abc01.
ssrf-via-zero-ip-bypass identifies missing IsUnspecified() check in server/audio/host_validator.go:57 as the root cause, which is an SSRF bypass, not a missing authentication flaw.
